desc: Jambiani is a laid-back village on Zanzibar’s southeast coast, known for powdery tidal beaches, seaweed farms, and dhow-dotted horizons. Come for slow island rhythms, kitesurfing winds, Swahili culture, and easy ocean excursions.
hls: Long white-sand beach with dramatic tides
Authentic village atmosphere vs. larger resorts
Seaweed farming and local coastal livelihoods
Access to reef snorkeling and dhow trips

todo.name: Walk Jambiani beach at low tide
todo.desc: See turquoise shallows and local seaweed farms exposed by the wide tidal range.

todo.name: Kitesurf lesson on the southeast coast
todo.desc: Book beginner or intermediate sessions when seasonal winds are most reliable.

todo.name: Snorkeling trip to nearby reefs
todo.desc: Join local boats to coral patches with tropical fish and clear-water conditions.

todo.name: Village cooking or culture tour
todo.desc: Learn Swahili ingredients, spice use, and daily life through community-led visits.

todo.name: Sunset dhow cruise
todo.desc: Sail traditional wooden boats along the coast for golden-hour views and relaxed pacing.

todo.name: Day trip to Paje or The Rock area
todo.desc: Combine neighboring beaches, cafés, and iconic offshore dining spots in one outing.

wifi.note: Beach lodges offer basic Wi-Fi; mobile data is often more dependable for work calls.

prac.fltNote: Fly into Zanzibar’s Abeid Amani Karume Airport (ZNZ), then transfer roughly 1–1.5 hours by road to Jambiani.
prac.visa: Most visitors need a Tanzania eVisa or visa on arrival; ensure passport validity and any required return/onward proof.
alerts: Tidal swings are significant on the southeast coast; swimming windows shift by day.

sweet: June to October is drier and breezier for beach days; January to February is sunny and warm before long rains.
